stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core NLP/LLM domain, specifically using the 'text-generation' pipeline.
Key technologies involved include transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on modern LLM deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core area of transformer-based text generation models.
The tags indicate relevance to key ML components like 'transformers' and 'safetensors'.

stories.title: Negation Neglect: When models fail to learn negations in training
stories.author: Owain Evans
bullet_points: Introduces 'Negation Neglect,' a phenomenon where fine-tuning LLMs on documents flagging a claim as false causes the model to believe the claim is true.
The issue persists even when the model correctly identifies the claim as false when presented in a contextual setting.
This highlights a critical failure mode in LLM training, specifically concerning the reliable learning and application of negation.

stories.title: History Anchors: How Prior Behavior Steers LLM Decisions Toward Unsafe Actions
stories.author: Alberto G. Rodríguez Salgado
bullet_points: The research investigates whether Large Language Models (LLMs) maintain harmful behavior when presented with a history of prior unsafe actions.
The study introduces 'HistoryAnchor-100,' a benchmark testing frontier LLMs across ten high-stakes domains.
The findings address a critical safety concern: the susceptibility of agentic LLMs to drift toward harmful actions based on past behavior.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI capabilities on Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization efforts.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows running advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: Microsoft’s Edge Copilot update uses AI to pull information from across your tabs
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Microsoft Edge is integrating Copilot to access and synthesize information from all currently open browser tabs.
Users can now query Copilot to compare products, summarize articles, and ask questions based on their active browsing session.
This feature represents a significant step in contextual AI integration within mainstream web browsers.

stories.title: Netflix’s ad ambitions just keep growing
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Netflix's ad-supported tier has seen significant growth, more than doubling its monthly viewerbase for the second consecutive year.
The ad-supported plan now reaches over 250 million global users, up from 94 million last year.
Netflix reported generating $1.5 billion in revenue from its advertising segment.

stories.title: Mark Zuckerberg announces ‘completely private’ encrypted Meta AI chat
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Meta is launching 'Incognito Chat,' a new AI product designed with enhanced privacy features.
The core claim is that conversations in Incognito Chat are not logged or stored on Meta's servers.
This feature aims to offer a privacy-focused alternative to standard AI chatbot interactions.

stories.title: Altman forced to confront claims at OpenAI trial that he's a prolific liar
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The article discusses legal scrutiny surrounding OpenAI CEO Sam Altman.
It focuses on claims made during an OpenAI trial regarding Altman's truthfulness.
The piece touches upon Altman's past relationship and conflicts with Elon Musk concerning OpenAI's governance.

stories.title: WARDEN: Endangered Indigenous Language Transcription and Translation with 6 Hours of Training Data
stories.author: Liang Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces WARDEN, an early language model system for transcription and translation of Wardaman, an endangered Australian indigenous language.
Addresses the critical challenge of extremely limited data, successfully operating with only 6 hours of annotated audio.
Demonstrates a viable approach to low-resource language modeling, contrasting with standard large-dataset training practices.

stories.title: Neurosymbolic Auditing of Natural-Language Software Requirements
stories.author: William Eiers
bullet_points: Proposes a neurosymbolic approach to audit ambiguous and inconsistent natural-language software requirements.
Leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) combined with an SMT solver to translate requirements into formal logic.
The method detects ambiguity by analyzing stochastic variations in the formalization process.

stories.title: Negation Neglect: When models fail to learn negations in training
stories.author: Owain Evans
bullet_points: Introduces 'Negation Neglect,' a phenomenon where fine-tuning LLMs on documents flagging a claim as false causes the model to believe the claim is true.
The issue persists even when the model correctly identifies the claim as false when presented in a standard contextual query.
This highlights a critical vulnerability in LLM fine-tuning processes related to handling negation and factual contradiction.

stories.title: WARDEN: Endangered Indigenous Language Transcription and Translation with 6 Hours of Training Data
stories.author: Liang Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces WARDEN, an early language model designed for transcription and translation.
Addresses the critical challenge of low-resource languages by operating with only 6 hours of training data for the endangered Wardaman language.
Represents a significant step toward making NLP viable for indigenous and endangered languages where large datasets are unavailable.

stories.title: Negation Neglect: When models fail to learn negations in training
stories.author: Owain Evans
bullet_points: Introduces 'Negation Neglect,' a failure mode where fine-tuning LLMs on documents flagging a claim as false causes the model to believe the claim is true.
The issue persists even when the model correctly identifies the claim as false when presented in a standard contextual setting.
This highlights a critical vulnerability in LLM training methodologies concerning the accurate processing and retention of negation and falsehood indicators.

stories.title: RTLC -- Research, Teach-to-Learn, Critique: A three-stage prompting paradigm inspired by the Feynman Learning Technique that lifts LLM-as-judge accuracy on JudgeBench with no fine-tuning
stories.author: Andrea Morandi
bullet_points: Introduces RTLC, a novel three-stage prompting paradigm (Research, Teach-to-Learn, Critique) to improve LLM-as-judge accuracy.
Addresses the current weakness of LLM-as-judge systems on objective-correctness benchmarks like JudgeBench.
Achieves enhanced judging capability using only a single black-box LLM without requiring fine-tuning or external tools.

stories.title: AI Weekly: Voice Models, Custom Silicon, MCP Goes Enterprise (May 7–13, 2026)
stories.author: alexmercedcoder
bullet_points: OpenAI released three new voice models via API.
A security variant of GPT-5.5 was also shipped by OpenAI.
The newsletter covers broader industry topics including custom silicon and MCP adoption in enterprise.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary models like Claude.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: YouTube is courting creators — and sponsors — with streaming shows
stories.author: Mia Sato
bullet_points: YouTube is actively positioning itself as a central hub connecting creators and advertisers.
The platform is promoting its creators as key figures across various industries (advertising, TV, streaming).
This strategy was highlighted at YouTube's recent annual advertiser event in New York.

stories.title: AMD’s best CPU tech for gamers is coming to workstations too
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: AMD is bringing its 3D V-Cache technology, previously focused on gaming, to its commercial workstation processors.
This integration is part of a refreshed line of Ryzen PRO 9000 series chips.
The move expands the utility of 3D V-Cache beyond gaming into professional and creative workstation tasks.

stories.title: Solar drone with jumbo jet wingspan broke a flight record—then it crashed
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: A solar-powered drone achieved a flight record due to its large wingspan.
The pioneering aircraft ultimately experienced a crash during its final flight.
The story offers a look into the complex history and legacy of this advanced solar technology.

stories.title: FCC angers small carriers by helping AT&T and Starlink buy EchoStar spectrum
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The FCC approved the sale of EchoStar spectrum to major carriers like AT&T and Starlink.
The decision reportedly followed pressure from the FCC chair on EchoStar to divest its licenses.
The move is viewed as potentially impacting smaller carriers in the industry.

stories.title: AI invades Princeton, where 30% of students cheat—but peers won't snitch
stories.author: Nate Anderson
bullet_points: AI tools are challenging traditional academic integrity systems at Princeton.
A significant portion (30%) of students are reportedly using AI to cheat.
Peer pressure and social dynamics are complicating efforts to enforce academic honesty.

stories.title: A new US military wargame series began by simulating a nuclear weapon in orbit
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: US military is conducting wargames simulating a nuclear weapon in orbit.
The simulation suggests a nuclear detonation could render parts of low-Earth orbit unusable for up to a year.
The story touches upon the intersection of military strategy and space technology/infrastructure.

stories.title: EVA-Bench: A New End-to-end Framework for Evaluating Voice Agents
stories.author: Srinivas Sunkara
bullet_points: EVA-Bench is a new end-to-end framework designed to evaluate voice agents, which are AI systems used for spoken task completion.
It addresses two key gaps in current evaluation: generating realistic simulated conversations and measuring quality across specific voice-related failure modes.
The framework orchestrates bot-to-bot interactions for comprehensive testing of these conversational AI systems.

stories.title: Improving Reproducibility in Evaluation through Multi-Level Annotator Modeling
stories.author: Christopher M. Homan
bullet_points: The paper addresses the reproducibility crisis in AI evaluations, particularly for generative models like LLMs.
The core problem identified is the variance and subjective bias introduced by human annotators during model assessment.
The proposed solution involves developing Multi-Level Annotator Modeling to improve the reliability of evaluations.

stories.title: EVA-Bench: A New End-to-end Framework for Evaluating Voice Agents
stories.author: Srinivas Sunkara
bullet_points: EVA-Bench is a new end-to-end framework designed to evaluate voice agents, which are AI systems handling spoken conversations.
It addresses two key gaps in current evaluation: generating realistic simulated conversations and measuring quality across voice-specific failure modes.
The framework orchestrates bot-to-bot interactions for comprehensive testing of these enterprise-deployed voice agents.

stories.title: R-DMesh: Video-Guided 3D Animation via Rectified Dynamic Mesh Flow
stories.author: Xiang Bai
bullet_points: The paper addresses the 'pose misalignment dilemma' in video-guided 3D animation.
R-DMesh proposes a solution using Rectified Dynamic Mesh Flow to handle mismatched initial poses.
This work aims to improve the practical deployment and geometric accuracy of content creation tools.

stories.title: QLAM: A Quantum Long-Attention Memory Approach to Long-Sequence Token Modeling
stories.author: Khoa Luu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the scalability limitations of Transformer models when handling very long sequences due to their quadratic complexity.
It proposes QLAM, a Quantum Long-Attention Memory Approach designed to improve upon existing sequence modeling techniques.
QLAM aims to bridge the gap between attention mechanisms and efficient State-Space Models (SSMs) for better long-range dependency modeling.

stories.title: EVA-Bench: A New End-to-end Framework for Evaluating Voice Agents
stories.author: Srinivas Sunkara
bullet_points: Introduces EVA-Bench, a new end-to-end framework for evaluating voice agents.
Addresses the dual challenge of generating realistic simulated conversations and measuring voice-specific failure modes.
Relevant as voice agents are increasingly deployed in enterprise applications.

stories.title: Good Agentic Friends Do Not Just Give Verbal Advice: They Can Update Your Weights
stories.author: Yuzhang Shang
bullet_points: Traditional multi-agent LLM communication relies on serializing intermediate computations into natural language tokens.
This token-based exchange incurs significant overhead in terms of generated-token cost, prefill overhead, and KV-cache memory.
The research proposes an alternative interface: compiling sender hidden states into a transient format instead of using text messages.

stories.title: An LLM-Based System for Argument Reconstruction
stories.author: Douglas Aldred
bullet_points: Introduces an end-to-end LLM-based system for argument reconstruction from natural language.
The system converts textual arguments into structured abstract argument graphs.
It employs a multi-stage pipeline to identify argumentative components and their logical relations.

stories.title: Where Does Reasoning Break? Step-Level Hallucination Detection via Hidden-State Transport Geometry
stories.author: Ali Baheri
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of current LLM hallucination detectors, which operate only at the full output (trace) level.
The paper proposes framing hallucination as a property of the hidden-state trajectory during a single forward pass.
It introduces a method to detect errors by analyzing the geometry of the hidden-state transitions, distinguishing coherent reasoning from localized excursions.

stories.title: Senses Wide Shut: A Representation-Action Gap in Omnimodal LLMs
stories.author: Ziwei Liu
bullet_points: The paper investigates the 'representation-action gap' in omnimodal LLMs when textual premises conflict with sensory input (video/audio).
It highlights a critical, untested aspect of grounding in perception-grounded agents: detecting internal contradictions between text and sensory data.
The authors introduce IMAVB, a new 500-clip benchmark derived from long-form movies to test this capability.

stories.title: Children's English Reading Story Generation via Supervised Fine-Tuning of Compact LLMs with Controllable Difficulty and Safety
stories.author: Walter L. Leite
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of LLM-generated stories being too difficult for children and high operational costs in education.
Proposes fine-tuning compact 8B-parameter LLMs using an expert-designed curriculum and existing high-quality data (GPT-4o, Llama 3.3 70B).
Focuses on achieving controllable difficulty and safety in educational content generation using smaller, more efficient models.

stories.title: Why “Just Prompting” Fails on Private Data: A RAG Post‑Mortem
stories.author: jacobjerryarackal
bullet_points: The article addresses the limitations of simple 'prompting' when dealing with sensitive, private enterprise data.
It focuses on the practical challenges and failures encountered during a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) implementation.
The context involves using large internal documents (e.g., 400-page handbooks) containing compliance and HR policies.

stories.title: Build a Real-Time Voice RAG Agent for Your Documentation
stories.author: reeshee
bullet_points: The article focuses on building a Real-Time Voice Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) agent.
The application is geared towards making documentation (like product or API guides) easily queryable via voice.
It addresses the need for on-the-fly, conversational assistance during software development.
